findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated styling/theme metadata and external font import not present in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML head contains invented content such as `<title>Converted Document</title>`, CSS comments `/* Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS */`, and `@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/...')`. These do not appear anywhere in the PDF pages, which only contain the form instructions.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is inconsistent and skips levels in multiple places.
findings.evidence: HTML uses `h2` for `General Instructions`, then `h3` for `Part 1 - Fuel Accountability`, then jumps to `h4` for line items such as `Line 1 - Beginning physical inventory`. Later sections repeat irregular patterns (`h3` for `Schedule of Receipts`, `h4` for `Schedule 1 - Gallons Received Tax-Paid`). While the PDF visually distinguishes headings, the HTML structure is not a clean semantic hierarchy.

findings.description: At least one sentence appears textually altered from the PDF, changing a line reference and likely introducing an accuracy error.
findings.evidence: In the PDF under Line 18, step 1 reads: `If Line 8 equals zero, calculate the administrative discount by multiplying Line 29 by .001, then multiply by the applicable road and inspection tax rates...` The HTML reproduces this, but this appears inconsistent with the line logic around Line 8/Line 7 comparisons and may reflect OCR/conversion ambiguity. More concretely, the HTML includes punctuation anomalies such as `not to exceed a total of $8,000., or,` in Line 18(3)(b), indicating text corruption from conversion.
